scenario.name: IEEE_Modulation
scenario.description: Scenario C — AM Sideband / LFO Oscillation Immunity Test (10% depth, 2 Hz)

params.standard: IEEE Std 60255-118-1:2018 §5.3.5 (AM modulation test, kx=0.1); IEEE C37.118.1-2011 §5.4.3; IEC 61000-4-7 (modulation products).
params.physical_motivation: Lightly-damped inter-area electromechanical oscillations at 0.1–3 Hz are characteristic of high-IBR transmission corridors (WECC, ENTSO-E system studies). IBR plants without POD controls excite these modes.
params.ibr_relevance: GFL-IBR with inadequate virtual inertia or POD can excite or sustain inter-area oscillations. Amplitude modulation at 2 Hz represents a lightly-damped (ζ~0.05) electromechanical mode — amplitude at the stability boundary before oscillatory instability.
params.parameters: v(t) = (1 + 0.1·cos(2π·2.0·t))·sin(2π·60·t); AM depth kx=0.1 (10%), modulation f_m=2.0 Hz. True frequency: constant 60.0 Hz.
params.harmonics: AM sidebands at 58 Hz and 62 Hz (±f_m = ±2 Hz about carrier). Per IEEE 519-2022: THD = 0% (sidebands are not integer harmonics). Not interharmonics (modulation product, not asynchronous source).
params.dynamics: Constant 60 Hz frequency; sinusoidal amplitude envelope. Estimators with AM-FM coupling produce spurious ~0.2 Hz peak frequency oscillation (SOGI-FLL, SRF-PLL) — the primary discrimination mechanism.
params.exceeds_standard: kx=0.1 is the IEC 60255-118-1 §5.3.5 standard test point. Pre-cascade IBR oscillation events (e.g., WECC 2020) exhibited modulation depths of 15–25% prior to instability — 1.5–2.5× the test level.
